titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5954

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=788 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.825856+00 | translation moa=789 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.837177+00

p.cl: Omnibus Christi fidelibus Has literas visuris uel Audituris Bertramus de parua Ristona salutem.

me intuitu caritatis et pro salute anime mee Antece
ssorum et successorum meorum Dedisse. et Concesisse . et hac presenti carta mea Confirmasse Deo et sancto Cvhtberto priori et monachis de Coldingham ibidem deo seruientibus et in
perpetuum seruituris vnam dimidiam carucatam terre In Campo et Territorio parue Ristone in liberam. puram. et perpetuam elemosinam silicet Duas accras terre et dimidiam In Tofto et Crofto in quo sedi Aliquando tempore patris mei. et Totam terram meam ad Ralfesaccres et Thorn. et vnam accram terre propinquiorem prati Rogeri
et Ricardi fili Iosep Hunderhecsyde extendentem se uersus occidentem. et Totam terram meam iacentem in mora ad australi parte de Blackechest’. et Totam
terram meam ad Blakechloeshefed excepta terra tantum Dauid de Drilton’. et Totam terram meam in Blackelawesflat. et omnem terram meam in Wilkedenflat
excepta terra tantum Dauid de Drilton’. et Totam terram meam in orientali parte de Stublisyde . et Totum Dominicum meum iacens in Duabus partibus in Hebr
andes castel. et vnam accram terre ad Grengate uersus occidentem. et Totam terram meam in Thyrstlislad quam Patricius filius Wtredi de me quondam tenuit. et
omnem friscam meam desuper iacentem. et Totam terram meam quam Ada Cocus quondam de me tenuit exceptis quatuor accriis terre quas Prior Dunelmensis
habet de Eadem terra in Eadem villa. et Totam terram meam in Tepermer. et quatuor accras terre et dimidiam in occidentali parte ad Morcroc. Omnes
istas terras superecriptas Dedi et hac presenti carta mea Confirmaui prefatis priori et monachis de Coldingham pro vna dimidia carucata terre in liberam. puram.
et perpetuam elemosinam. Renuncians in hoc facto pro me et heredibus meiis omni Iuris Ausilio canonici et ciuilis quod Dicti prior et monachi de Coldingham
nullo tempore pro hac pura. et perpetua elemosina in foro laicali trahantur nec vexentur. In moris et maricis. In pratis et pasturis. In petariis et turbariis. In
viis et semitis. et In omnibus aliis assiamentis ad Eandem pertinentibus terram infra villam et extra. Habendas et Tenendas Dictis priori et monachis de Coldin
gham de me et heredibus meiis inperpetuum adeo libere. quiete. et honorifice In omnibus sicut aliqua elemosina infra Regnum Scocie liberius aut quiecius tenetur
uel possidetur. Ego uero Bertramus et heredes mei predictam dimidiam carucatam terre cum Dicto tofto et Crofto et cum omnibus supradictis particulis terre
et cum omnibus suis pertinenciis infra villam et extra sepedictis priori et monachis de Coldingham contra omnes homines et feminas in perpetuum warantizabimus. adquie
tabimus. et deffendemus.
In Cuius Rei Testimonium huic scripto sigillum meum appossui.

Hiis Testibus Domino Willelmo de Mordingtona. Domino Alano de
Swyntona. Thoma de Nesbyt. Dauid de Lumesden. Roberto filio Gregorii de Coldingham. Ada de Prendergest’. Patricio Scoto. Ada filio Iohannis de Super
ioriaytona. Gregorio de Bethyoc. Gilberto de Lumesden. Roberto filio Reginaldi. Rogero filio Ade de magna Ristona et ALIIS.
To all Christ's faithful who shall view or hear these letters, Bertram of Little Reston sends greeting.

out of charity and for the salvation of my own soul, and of my ancestors' and successors' souls, to have given, and granted, and to have made firm by this my present charter, to God and to St Cuthbert, to the prior, and to the monks of Coldingham serving God in that place now and in the future, for ever, a half-ploughgate of land in the cultivated fields and territory of Little Reston, in pure and everlasting alms; that is, two-and-a-half acres of land in the toft and croft in which I settled at some time in my father's day; and all my land at Ralfesaccres and Thorn; and one acre of land nearest the meadow belonging to Roger and Richard, sons of Joseph Hunderhecsyde, extending westwards; and all my land lying in the moor, from the south side of Blackechest'; and all my land at Blackechloeshefed, saving only the land of David of Drilton; and all my land in Blackelawesflat; and all my land in Wilkedenflat, saving only that of David of Drilton; and all my land on the eastern side of Stublisyde; and my whole demesne lying on the two sides of Hebrandes Castle; and one acre of land at Grengate towards the west; and my whole land in Thyrslislad which Patrick son of Uhtred once held of me, and all my uncultivated land lying thereon; and my whole land which Adam Cook once held of me, except for four acres of land which the Prior of Durham had of the same in the same toun; and all my land in Tepermer; and four-and-a-half acres of land on the western side at Morcroc. All these lands written above I have given and made firm by this, my present charter, to the aforementioned prior and monks of Coldingham, in return for a half-ploughgate of land, in free, pure, and everlasting alms, renouncing by this action, for my own part and for my heirs, every aid of canon and civil law, in order that the said prior and monks of Coldingham at no time for this pure and everlasting alms may be brought to a lay court nor be harassed; in moors and marshes, in meadows and pastures, in peataries and turbaries, in roads and paths, and in all other easements pertaining to the same land, within the toun and outside it; for the said prior and monks of Coldingham to have and to hold of me and my heirs for ever, as freely, quitely, and honourably in all things as any alms within the kingdom of Scotland is freely or quitely held or possessed. And I, Bertram, and my heirs, the aforesaid half-ploughgate of land, with the said toft and croft, and with all the above-said portions of land, and with all their pertinents within the toun and outside it, to the oft-mentioned prior and monks of Coldingham shall warrant, acquiet, and defend against all men and women for ever.
In testimony of this matter, I have applied my seal to this writing.

With these witnesses: the lord William of Mordington; the lord Alan of Swinton; Thomas of Nisbet; David of Lumsdaine; Robert son of Gregory of Coldingham; Adam of Prenderguest; Patrick Scot; Adam son of John of Over Ayton; Gregory of Beoch; Gilbert of Lumsdaine; Robert son of Reginald; Roger son of Adam of Great Reston; and others.
